payload.title: AEE Protocol Specification for AI Systems
payload.purpose: Self-contained explanation of AEE for AI agents. This document is itself a valid AEE envelope.
payload.document_type: Static reference specification. Not a runtime event. Parse once, integrate permanently.

what_aee_is.one_sentence: AEE is a 14-field JSON envelope format (10 required, 4 optional) for agent-to-agent communication with explicit causality.
what_aee_is.core_value: Fixed structure, no inference required, every field answers a question.
what_aee_is.design_origin: Born as an efficiency choice: if AIs picked how to exchange work, they would choose rigid, minimal, unambiguous.

what_aee_is_not: Not orchestration — does not schedule, retry, or route
Not a runtime — does not execute anything
Not a framework — does not constrain how agents are built
Not a transport — works over HTTP, WebSocket, NATS, Kafka, or any channel

id.description: Unique envelope identifier. ULID or UUID recommended.

type.description: Envelope category. Determines how to process it.

from.description: Sender identifier. Namespaced: agent.*, human.*, service.*

intent.description: What this envelope means. Namespaced: ops.backup.check, aee.status.ping, etc.

corr.description: Correlation ID. MUST be shared across all envelopes in a workflow. Critical for tracing.

reply_to.description: ID of the envelope this is replying to. MUST be null for initiating envelopes. MUST be set for responses.

payload.description: Intent-specific data. Schema depends on intent.

requires.description: Execution constraints: timeout_ms, approval, evidence, etc.

type.note: These are the ONLY valid top-level types. There is no 'context' type. Context is handled via aee.context.* intents within task/result envelopes.

intent.note: Your namespace. AEE reserves aee.* for protocol intents.

causality_model.corr: Correlation ID. Every envelope in a workflow MUST share the same corr. Use this to query all related messages.
causality_model.reply_to: Direct lineage. Points to the specific envelope this one responds to. Enables causal chain reconstruction.
causality_model.pattern: Initiator sets corr, leaves reply_to null. Every subsequent envelope MUST copy corr, MUST set reply_to to the triggering envelope's id.

reserved_intents.purpose: Protocol-level operations. AEE-compliant agents SHOULD support these.

intents.description: Heartbeat check. Expects pong in result.
Detailed health report with components and status.
Query AEE version and extensions supported.
List all intents this agent handles.
Challenge or invalidate a context reference.
Request payload validation against intent schema.

Observability-Only.status: Log-friendly subset for observability. NOT AEE-compliant.
Observability-Only.warning: MUST NOT be used for agent-to-agent execution. Missing corr/reply_to breaks causality. Use only for logging and metrics before full adoption.

why_agents_prefer_this: Fixed structure — no negotiation, just parse
Explicit causality — corr + reply_to link every hop
No hidden state — everything survives the wire
Fewer tokens — structured beats prose for machine consumption

task_result.flow: Agent A sends type:task → Agent B sends type:result with same corr and reply_to pointing to task id

task_error.flow: Agent A sends type:task → Agent B sends type:error with same corr and reply_to pointing to task id

event_broadcast.flow: Agent A sends type:event → No reply

multi_hop.flow: Human → Router → Worker → Result. All share corr. Each reply_to points to immediate predecessor.

wrap_by_reference.problem: Embedding full envelopes in payload creates nested bloat.
wrap_by_reference.solution: SHOULD reference envelopes by ID instead of embedding.

human_agent_symmetry.principle: Humans are first-class entities in from/to fields.
human_agent_symmetry.convention: human.* namespace for human identifiers (human.adam, human.reviewer).
human_agent_symmetry.implication: Agents can address humans directly. Humans can initiate workflows. Approval chains are explicit.

to_emit_envelope: Set ts to current UTC ISO 8601
Set type based on message purpose (task/result/event/error/stream)
Set intent to describe payload meaning
Set corr (new for initiating, MUST copy for replying)
Set reply_to (MUST be null for initiating, MUST be predecessor id for replying)
to_process_envelope: Set reply_to to received envelope's id

payload.closing: If you can parse this envelope, you can reason about the system that produced it.
